nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
# Manage Webflow form submissions in Discord 
## Full guide with video
[Full guide with video here]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

This workflow dynamically creates Discord channels for your Webflow forms then sends form submissions to those channels. The Webflow form name is used to make the channel name.

## Getting started
1. Create Webflow credential using API V1 Token
2. Create Discord credentials using Bot API by making an application [Your applications in Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} for a detailed list of scopes for your application please see the video guide above.
3. Connect your credentials to the relevant nodes on the canvas.
4. Activate the workflow and submit a form on your Webflow site

That's it! You do not need to add any custom code to your Webflow forms or site.

The name of your forms in the form settings section of the Designer in Webflow will be used to create the Discord channels. This workflow will automatically do this for you.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
### Format the message 
Discord accepts Markdown

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
### False branch 
We create a new Discord channel using the form name in Webflow. Channel names must be converted to lowercase and words separated with dash.

When the new channel is created we send a message in the #general channel with a direct link to the new channel.

Finally we send the Webflow form submission as a message in the new channel.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note3

parameters.content:
### Combining data to move forward 
This code will check if a channel with the form name exists in Discord. 

We also create an object to pass forward to the next node.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
# Manage Webflow form submissions in Discord 
## Full guide with video
[Full guide with video here]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

This workflow dynamically creates Discord channels for your Webflow forms then sends form submissions to those channels. The Webflow form name is used to make the channel name.

## Getting started
1. Create Webflow credential using API V1 Token
2. Create Discord credentials using Bot API by making an application [Your applications in Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} for a detailed list of scopes for your application please see the video guide above.
3. Connect your credentials to the relevant nodes on the canvas.
4. Activate the workflow and submit a form on your Webflow site

That's it! You do not need to add any custom code to your Webflow forms or site.

The name of your forms in the form settings section of the Designer in Webflow will be used to create the Discord channels. This workflow will automatically do this for you.

### Format the message 
Discord accepts Markdown

### False branch 
We create a new Discord channel using the form name in Webflow. Channel names must be converted to lowercase and words separated with dash.

When the new channel is created we send a message in the #general channel with a direct link to the new channel.

Finally we send the Webflow form submission as a message in the new channel.

### Combining data to move forward 
This code will check if a channel with the form name exists in Discord. 

We also create an object to pass forward to the next node.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: discord Bot  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Webflow Form Submission Trigger".
Open the "Does the channel exist?" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "List Discord Channels" node (n8n-nodes-base.discord) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Discord channel with form name" node (n8n-nodes-base.discord) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Notify #general channel of newly created channel1" node (n8n-nodes-base.discord) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send form submission to Discord channel" node (n8n-nodes-base.discord) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Transform data to send message".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
